workloads.name: buffer-upload 1KB
workloads.description: Single upload command used to align with Dawn BufferUploadPerf WriteBuffer 1KB.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Matched single queue write upload shape on both sides; Fawn uses deferred queue sync to avoid per-command wait inflation at tiny payload size while preserving per-upload timing normalization.

timingNormalization.note: Strict upload comparability mode: run 500 uploads per sample, no ignore-first adjustment, submitEvery=1 on both sides, and normalize to per-upload units.

timingNormalization.note: Strict upload comparability mode: run 500 uploads per sample, no ignore-first adjustment, submitEvery=1 on both sides, and normalize to per-upload units.

timingNormalization.note: Strict upload comparability mode: run 100 uploads per sample, no ignore-first adjustment, submitEvery=1 on both sides, and normalize to per-upload units.

workloads.name: render draw throughput
workloads.description: Native render/draw throughput workload aligned to Dawn DrawCallPerf base variant.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Dawn DrawCallPerf reports per draw (iterationsPerStep=2000); Fawn render_draw normalizes to per-draw units.

reasons: left p50 timing (36.0ns) is below the 100ns measurement noise floor
left p50 timing (36.0ns) is below the 100ns measurement noise floor

workloads.name: render redundant pipeline/bindings

timingNormalization.note: Dawn DrawCallPerf redundant-pipeline variant reports per draw (iterationsPerStep=2000); Fawn repeats the redundant-pipeline command stream 10x and normalizes to per-draw units to reduce sample-tail setup jitter.

workloads.name: texture sampler/write/query/destroy contract
workloads.description: Texture API contract benchmark covering sampler creation, queue write texture, texture query assertions, and resource destroy lifecycle.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Fawn command stream executes sampler/texture lifecycle calls plus render draws; repeat=10 with deferred queue sync and per-iteration normalization reduce fixed setup/sync skew while keeping SubresourceTrackingPerf-oriented units.

workloads.name: texture sampler/write/query/destroy contract (mip8)
workloads.description: Texture API contract benchmark aligned to SubresourceTrackingPerf mipLevel=8 variant.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Normalized per iteration against the SubresourceTrackingPerf mipLevel=8 variant with repeat=10 and deferred queue sync to amortize fixed setup/sync overhead.

workloads.name: async pipeline diagnostics contract
workloads.description: Async diagnostics benchmark covering async render pipeline creation, error scopes, and shader compilation info.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Apples-to-apples only: Fawn repeats async diagnostics command and normalizes by async sub-steps to reduce fixed process/setup skew.

workloads.name: p1 resource-table/immediates macro (500)
workloads.description: Apples-to-apples macro ResourceTable workload repeating resource-table/immediates path 500 times inside one command.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Apples-to-apples macro ResourceTable contract: command-repeat=500 with per-iteration normalization and explicit feature-gating parity support.

workloads.name: texture contract macro (500 draws)
workloads.description: Apples-to-apples macro texture lifecycle workload with a larger render-draw phase for setup amortization analysis.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Apples-to-apples macro workload: normalize to per-iteration units over a 500-draw texture lifecycle command stream.

workloads.name: p0 resource lifecycle contract
workloads.description: Apples-to-apples contract workload for upload-buffer resize churn plus explicit barrier submission.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Apples-to-apples contract. Dawn filter mapping and repeat accounting are explicitly aligned for strict claim lanes; uses deferred queue sync on Metal to avoid per-command wait inflation.

timingNormalization.note: Apples-to-apples lifecycle contract: normalize per command-sequence iteration over 500 upload resize + barrier operations with deferred queue sync on Metal.

workloads.name: p0 render pixel-local-storage barrier macro (500)
workloads.description: Apples-to-apples macro PLS workload with 500 in-command iterations to stress non-coherent barrier sequencing overhead.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Apples-to-apples macro PLS contract: command-repeat=500 with per-iteration barrier-sequencing normalization and explicit feature-gating parity support.

workloads.name: concurrent execution single-dispatch contract
workloads.description: Compute contract aligned to Dawn ConcurrentExecutionTest RunSingle with a single heavy compute dispatch.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Promoted to strict comparable on local Metal using full-suite evidence from bench/out/scratch/20260226T005744Z/metal.full.fixed.full12.json and retained under apples-to-apples operation timing policy with zero execution error/unsupported requirements.

timingNormalization.note: Dawn ConcurrentExecutionTest RunSingle trace evidence in strict lanes exposes one physical operation per timed sample on this lane; rightTimingDivisor is fixed to 1.0 for apples-to-apples normalization.

workloads.name: uniform buffer update (WriteBuffer partial single)
workloads.description: Uniform update + draw benchmark aligned to Dawn UniformBufferUpdatePerf WriteBuffer Partial SingleUniformBuffer variant.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Apples-to-apples contract: per-iteration upload+draw workflow and normalization aligned to Dawn UniformBufferUpdatePerf WriteBuffer/Partial/SingleUniformBuffer.

timingNormalization.note: Dawn UniformBufferUpdatePerf reports per-iteration units; this Metal lane currently emits 200 physical operations per timed sample for this contract, so strict normalization uses leftTimingDivisor=200.
